images.caption: Figure 1:An example of the financial audit verification.

Figure 3:Main results across methods and backbone models. Panel (a) reports Joint ACC (%), where higher is better. Panels (b) to (d) report structural error rate (SER), extraction error rate (EER), and calculation error rate (CER), where lower is better.

Figure 4:(a) Ablation results on GPT-5.5. Rule columns report per-rule Joint ACC; Invalid is the structurally unusable output rate. (b) Tool-use behavior across backbones.A1A_{1}uses static taxonomy tools only, so its static/dynamic split is omitted.A2A_{2}consistently mixes taxonomy and filing tools, while the tool budget varies by backbone.

Figure 5:Temporal tool-use profile of the Forensic AuditorA2A_{2}. Each point shows the share of dynamic filing-environment calls at tool stepkk. All backbones follow an evidence-first, checks-last pattern, with different switchover depths. Fino1-14B is omitted because it emits no well-formed tool calls.
